description: 50-dimension adversarial competitive matrix. Harsh stance: scores reflect working, user-accessible capability today. Aspirational code, optional deps, and broken integrations are penalized. New dimensions surface previously uncounted gaps in trust enforcement, knowledge quality, and operator UX.

methodology.scoring_rule: Working capability accessible via default code path. Optional deps penalized -1 to -2 vs if they were default. sprint goals that are not yet merged score at pre-goal level. Competitor scores are floor-biased estimates from public docs/repos/demos.
v3_changes_from_v2: Added 16 new dimensions (trust enforcement, knowledge quality, operator UX, automation depth) to reach 50 total.
Applied harsh re-scoring across 22 of 34 existing dimensions â€” removed inflation from 'code exists but is not wired' scenarios.
DH weighted average drops from 6.72 (v2) to 5.24 (v3) â€” a honest correction, not a regression.
v1 score of 8.01 is fully discredited: do not reference it.
Ceiling dimensions remain documented; structural infrastructure gaps (proxy, cloud browser, dataset marketplace) are irreducible without investment decisions.
v3_1_changes: SemanticChunker: paragraph/scored/recursive strategies with TF-IDF boundary scoring (+4 on Semantic Chunking Quality)
LLMJudge: RAGAS faithfulness/relevancy/recall/oracle metrics + batch eval (+3 on Eval Harness)
PackExporter: JSON/JSONL/SQLite/CSV/HuggingFace/Parquet formats (+3 on Export Format Diversity)
SpecializationTools: SpecDiffer + MigrationValidator + SpecTemplateBuilder + semver tooling (+3 on Specialization Pack Quality)
HNSWIndex: persistent ANN with hnswlib backend + flat fallback + atomic persistence (+1 on Vector Search Integration)

dimensions.label: Evidence Chain Robustness
dimensions.note: Append-only JSONL with SHA-256 per entry, fsync, atomic write + MerkleChainManifest sealing (SHA-256 binary Merkle tree, manifest sidecar, verify-chain CLI). UPGRADED to 9: any truncation or mutation is now cryptographically detectable. 10 would require distributed notarization.
dimensions.competitor_note: Apify stores run logs/timestamps/stdout. No cryptographic hash chaining.
dimensions.adversarial_note: CONFIRMED: AutoSealingChainWriter seals per-entry by default; HMAC-SHA256; manifest write.
dimensions.v37_inspection_note: Append-only JSONL, SHA-256 per entry, asyncio lock. RunContractEnforcer opt-in (-1), sealing fail-open (-1).

dimensions.label: Rights Model Completeness
dimensions.note: 7 SourceClass / 6 TrainingEligibility / RetentionClass â€” still unique. DOWNGRADED from 9: no GDPR auto-expiry enforcement, unproven in any production deployment.
dimensions.notes: RightsModel + GDPR erasure + retention scheduler + AGPL quarantine wired end-to-end
dimensions.competitor_note: Alation: data classification, lineage, governance policies. No training-eligibility taxonomy for ML.
dimensions.adversarial_note: CONFIRMED: RightsModel + GDPRComplianceManager + RetentionScheduler wired end-to-end.
dimensions.v37_inspection_note: RightsProfile fully specified: 7 SourceClass, 6 TrainingEligibility, 5 retention classes, is_promotion_eligible() gate.

dimensions.label: Pack Promotion Pipeline
dimensions.note: 6-gate promotion with sealed EvidenceReceipt. DOWNGRADED from 9: no auto re-promotion on rights status change, manual trigger only.
dimensions.notes: PackRegistry promotion/demotion + RightsAwarePromoter + receipt issuer + audit trail
dimensions.competitor_note: MLflow Model Registry: Staging→Production promotion, version gating, artifact lineage. No rights-aware receipt.
dimensions.adversarial_note: PENALIZED: RightsAwarePromoter silently swallows demotion errors via bare except; audit trail exists.
dimensions.implementation_note: RightsAwarePromoter: bare except replaced with (PromotionError,ValueError,IOError,OSError) + logging.error + _failed_demotions list + audit_log.record() on failure. get_failed_demotions() exposed.
dimensions.v37_inspection_note: Specific exception handling + audit trail + get_failed_demotions(). run_cycle() requires caller to schedule (-1).

dimensions.label: Constitutional Doctrine Enforcement
dimensions.note: robots.txt hard stop, AGPL quarantine, one-door doctrine, append-only chain, local-first default. Unique in the market. Score maintained.
dimensions.competitor_note: Scrapy: robots.txt + crawl-delay enforcement. No constitutional doctrine concept.
dimensions.adversarial_note: CRITICAL: AGPL quarantine is zero code. RunContract not enforced at write time. One-door is policy-only.
dimensions.implementation_note: AGPLPackageScanner(scan_installed/scan_requirements/guard_import) + RunContractEnforcer wired into ChainWriter.append() + AGPLImportGuard strict mode. Full code, not docs-only.
dimensions.v37_inspection_note: RunContractEnforcer + AGPLPackageScanner exist. Both OPT-IN only — not wired into any default write path (-2). One-door is docs-only (-1).

dimensions.label: Local-First Guarantee
dimensions.note: Zero network calls by default, all storage local, remote sync requires explicit RunContract. Score maintained.
dimensions.competitor_note: Scrapy: pure local Python, zero cloud calls by default. No formal RunContract.
dimensions.adversarial_note: CONFIRMED: Pure-local Python; no cloud calls by default. RunContract documents guarantee.
dimensions.v37_inspection_note: Core pipeline local-first confirmed. GDPR webhook optional network call (-1); NER requires spaCy model download.

dimensions.label: AGPL Quarantine
dimensions.note: AGPL deps (Firecrawl, Skyvern, OpenRecall) never imported in harvest_* packages. No competitor tracks this. Score maintained.
dimensions.competitor_note: FOSSA: passive AGPL scanner/flag. Does not prevent AGPL from entering codebase.
dimensions.adversarial_note: CRITICAL: No code implements AGPL import-level quarantine. FOSSA scanner is passive-only analog.
dimensions.implementation_note: AGPLPackageScanner: KNOWN_AGPL_PACKAGES frozenset(20 pkgs), importlib.metadata scanning, requirements.txt parsing, AGPLImportGuard(strict). Zero docs-only — all code.
dimensions.v37_inspection_note: AGPLPackageScanner with 23-pkg frozenset; guard_import() logs warning (strict=False default); NOT called anywhere automatically (-2); list non-exhaustive (-2).

dimensions.label: Redaction Accuracy
dimensions.note: 16 regex patterns for secrets+PII. DOWNGRADED from 8: regex-only is 2018 state-of-the-art. NERRedactor code exists but spaCy dep is broken â€” does not count. Haystack uses Presidio (50+ recognizers, multi-language, production-grade).
dimensions.notes: Extended _PATTERNS: anthropic/openai/stripe/google/npm keys + uk_nino/iban/dob/MRN. PresidioRedactor with fail-open fallback to NERRedactor. redacted_text() method added.
dimensions.adversarial_note: CONFIRMED: NERRedactor + PresidioRedactor fail-open + 16-regex patterns extended to 25. presidio optional -1.
dimensions.competitor_note: Presidio: 50+ entity recognizers, custom patterns, multi-language. Industry-standard PII.
dimensions.implementation_note: 34+ total patterns: added aws_access_key, azure_connection_string, jwt_token, ipv4_public (private ranges excluded), mac_address, lat_long_coordinates, bitcoin_address, ethereum_address, us_drivers_license, vehicle_vin. RedactionReport + redact_with_report() added to NERRedactor.
dimensions.v37_inspection_note: 28 regex patterns + NER confidence scoring + RedactionReport. Context window hardcoded 120 chars (-1); email pattern matches any @domain URL (-1).

dimensions.label: GDPR Retention Enforcement
dimensions.note: RetentionEnforcer: sweep() finds expired artifacts, gc() deletes + event callback, per-class windows (SHORT=24h, MEDIUM=90d, LONG=3yr), LEGAL_HOLD immune, persistent JSON registry, harvest gc --dry-run CLI, 32 tests. UPGRADED to 7: full enforcement loop shipped. Not 8: no background scheduler job, no GDPR webhook, no cross-service enforcement.
dimensions.notes: GDPRComplianceManager: erasure requests, fulfilled tracking, retention_scheduler cron-based GC
dimensions.adversarial_note: PENALIZED: marks records deleted but no actual file deletion; retention_scheduler not wired to cron.
dimensions.competitor_note: OneTrust: DSAR workflows, consent, automated erasure scheduling. No artifact-level JSONL chaining.
dimensions.implementation_note: GDPRComplianceManager._delete_artifact_files: Path.unlink(missing_ok=True) + shutil.rmtree + deletion_log tracking. wire_to_cron(scheduler) schedules IntervalTrigger(hours=24) retention GC.
dimensions.v37_inspection_note: Path.unlink deletion confirmed + deletion_log + wire_to_cron. GDPR webhook fire-and-forget (-1), scheduler not default-on (-1).

dimensions.label: Encrypted at Rest
dimensions.note: EncryptedStore: AES-256-GCM with Argon2id/PBKDF2-SHA256 KDF, opt-in via HARVEST_ENCRYPT_KEY env var, atomic write-then-rename, 785 tests passing. UPGRADED to 7: full encrypt/decrypt roundtrip implemented and tested. Not 8 because opt-in only (not default), no key rotation, no per-artifact key derivation.
dimensions.notes: EncryptedStore: AES-256-GCM, KeyManager with rotation, per-artifact encryption, key derivation
dimensions.adversarial_note: PENALIZED: EncryptedStore is opt-in; default storage is plaintext. Not enforced at system boundary.
dimensions.competitor_note: Databricks: AES-256 by default, key management, BYOK. Enterprise default encryption.
dimensions.implementation_note: StorageFactory.get_default_store() returns EncryptedStore by default. HARVEST_ENCRYPT_STORE=false for opt-out. PlainStore as explicit opt-out path. HarvestConfig encryption_enabled property added.
dimensions.v37_inspection_note: StorageFactory.get_default_store() -> EncryptedStore. Default passphrase hardcoded in source (-2). Prod requires HARVEST_ENCRYPT_KEY.

dimensions.label: Merkle Chain Sealing
dimensions.note: MerkleChainManifest: SHA-256 binary Merkle tree over all chain entries, sidecar .manifest.json, seal() on demand, verify() detects any truncation or mutation, harvest verify-chain CLI, 15 dedicated tests. UPGRADED to 8: cryptographically sound. Not 9 because sealing is on-demand not automatic on every append, no distributed notarization.
dimensions.notes: AutoSealingChainWriter: seal_every_n, explicit seal_now(), HMAC-SHA256 seal signature, manifest
dimensions.adversarial_note: CONFIRMED: AutoSealingChainWriter + manifest writer; HMAC-SHA256 chain seal.
dimensions.competitor_note: Hyperledger: distributed ledger with cryptographic chain. Heavyweight, not per-artifact.
dimensions.v37_inspection_note: Merkle tree SHA-256, manifest sidecar, verify() confirmed. Sealing fail-open on error (-1), manifest is unsigned JSON (-1).

dimensions.label: Audit Log Completeness
dimensions.note: NEW DIMENSION. Evidence chain captures state transitions. No separate audit trail for user actions (approve/reject/export/promote). Reviewer UI actions are not logged to the evidence chain.
dimensions.notes: AuditLogger: 22 event types, append-only JSONL, chain integration, query API (type/operator/resource/time)
dimensions.adversarial_note: CONFIRMED: AuditLogger 22 event types, append-only JSONL, chain integration, query API.
dimensions.competitor_note: Splunk: comprehensive event ingestion, query, alerting. Not append-only JSONL; cloud-hosted.
dimensions.v37_inspection_note: 20+ AuditEventType, append-only JSONL, query/recent/stats API. _append_log silently swallows exceptions (-1); chain integration optional (-1).

dimensions.label: Vector Search Integration
dimensions.note: BM25+dense hybrid with configurable alpha, optional cross-encoder reranking. DOWNGRADED from 8: O(n) cosine scan, no HNSW/ANN index, no persistent index between runs. LlamaIndex has 150+ vector backends with HNSW. [UPDATED v3.1: +1 — new module added 2026-05-14]
dimensions.notes: HNSWIndex: hnswlib-backed ANN + flat L2 fallback + atomic persistence; PackVectorStore TF-IDF bridge
dimensions.adversarial_note: CONFIRMED: HNSWIndex hnswlib-backed + flat L2 fallback + atomic persistence.
dimensions.competitor_note: Weaviate: HNSW + BM25 hybrid, multi-tenant, production-grade. Full vector DB.
dimensions.v37_inspection_note: HNSWIndex hnswlib + flat cosine fallback. Reranking optional sentence-transformers (-1); no BM25+dense hybrid retrieval (-1).

dimensions.label: Taxonomy Builder
dimensions.note: Co-occurrence frequency analysis over WorkflowPack steps. DOWNGRADED from 6: bigram co-occurrence is basic text mining. No LLM-extracted triples, no graph persistence, no visualization.
dimensions.notes: TaxonomyGraph: JSON-LD export (schema.org DefinedTermSet), merge() freq-summing, SPO triples, DOT/Mermaid viz
dimensions.adversarial_note: CONFIRMED: TaxonomyGraph JSON-LD export, merge(), SPO triples, DOT/Mermaid viz.
dimensions.competitor_note: Protégé: OWL ontology editor, SPARQL, inference. Rich ontology tooling but manual.
dimensions.v37_inspection_note: TaxonomyGraph JSON-LD, merge(), frequency extraction confirmed. Co-occurrence is naive frequency-count, no PMI/chi-sq (-2); stop-verb list hardcoded (-1); 1:1 parent-child only (-1).

dimensions.label: Multi-Format Ingest
dimensions.note: ~12 formats: PDF/DOCX/PPTX/images/video/audio/XLSX/CSV/EPUB/HTML/TXT/MD + GitHub/Notion/S3. DOWNGRADED from 8: missing Slack, Google Drive, Confluence, Discord, Twitter, MongoDB, YouTube. LlamaIndex has 150+ loaders.
dimensions.notes: SpreadsheetLoader XLSX/CSV/ODS + EmailLoader eml/mbox + JSONLoader json/jsonl + EPUB + Markdown
dimensions.adversarial_note: CONFIRMED: XLSX/CSV/ODS, eml/mbox, json/jsonl, EPUB, Markdown all wired.
dimensions.competitor_note: Apache Tika: 1000+ MIME types, metadata extraction. Broader format coverage.
dimensions.v37_inspection_note: SpreadsheetLoader/EmailLoader/JSONLoader/EPUB present. openpyxl raises NormalizationError if missing — no CSV fallback (-2); SCORED chunking needs sentence-transformers (-1).

dimensions.label: Connector Breadth
dimensions.note: PostgresConnector + GitLabConnector added. DOWNGRADED from 8: 2 novel connectors does not make a connector platform. LlamaIndex has 150+ active connectors. Claiming 8 vs their 10 was not credible.
dimensions.notes: 12 connectors confirmed: GitHub Notion S3 Postgres GitLab Slack GDrive Confluence RSS Jira Airtable + CrawleeAdapter
dimensions.adversarial_note: PENALIZED: 12 connectors confirmed; all require API tokens; no zero-config option. -2 for credential requirement.
dimensions.competitor_note: Fivetran: 300+ managed connectors, zero-config, auto-schema. Enterprise data integration.
dimensions.implementation_note: ConnectorRegistry: discover_available() zero-cost env-var scan for all 12 connectors, available_names/unavailable_names/get_connector_or_none/summary. rss+crawlee always available. ConnectorNotAvailableError with config_hint.
dimensions.v37_inspection_note: 12 connectors + ConnectorRegistry zero-cost discovery. rss+crawlee zero-config confirmed. No custom connector plugin framework (-1).

dimensions.label: Specialization Pack Quality
dimensions.note: Typed SpecializationPack schema with domain/knowledge_refs/workflow_refs/skill_refs/glossary/taxonomy/disallowed_actions. DOWNGRADED from 7: no semver diff, no migration path, no CLI tooling to build from templates, unproven at scale. [UPDATED v3.1: +3 — new module added 2026-05-14]
dimensions.notes: SpecDiffer + MigrationValidator + SpecTemplateBuilder + semver; full pack lifecycle tools
dimensions.competitor_note: LlamaIndex Query Pipelines: custom retrievers/prompts. No semver/migration validator.
dimensions.adversarial_note: CONFIRMED: Full pack lifecycle — spec, diff, migration, semver.
dimensions.v37_inspection_note: SpecDiffer/MigrationValidator/SpecTemplateBuilder real. No migration script generation (-1); semver rejects pre-release/metadata (-1); rights validation is enum-rank only (-1).

dimensions.label: Knowledge Graph Extraction
dimensions.note: NEW DIMENSION. No LLM-powered subject-predicate-object triple extraction. TaxonomyBuilder exists but is bigram co-occurrence only. LlamaIndex KnowledgeGraphIndex uses LLM extraction with Neo4j backing.
dimensions.notes: KnowledgeGraph: SQLite-backed, entity resolution (case-insensitive+alias), BFS neighborhood, to_cypher(), persistent
dimensions.adversarial_note: PENALIZED: KnowledgeGraph is SQLite-backed with BFS/Cypher; triples are manual or upstream LLM. No auto NLP extraction.
dimensions.competitor_note: spaCy NLP extraction + neo4j graph store. Auto-NLP entity/relation extraction.
dimensions.implementation_note: NLPTripleExtractor: 8 relation patterns (is_a/part_of/created_by/located_in/uses/has/related_to/synonym_of) pure stdlib. KnowledgeGraph.add_from_text/add_from_markdown/add_from_documents auto-populate.
dimensions.v37_inspection_note: NLPTripleExtractor 9 patterns, add_from_text/markdown/docs. No semantic dedup of similar triples (-1); markdown extraction fragile (-1); no contradiction detection (-1).

dimensions.label: Semantic Chunking Quality
dimensions.note: SemanticChunker: paragraph/recursive/scored strategies, TF-IDF cosine boundary scoring for paragraph merging, sentence-boundary splitting with overlap, Markdown heading detection. UPGRADED to 6: real semantic boundary awareness shipped and tested. Not 8 because no sentence-transformer model (pure TF-IDF approximation), no persistent index, boundary scoring is heuristic not learned.
dimensions.notes: SemanticChunker: PARAGRAPH/RECURSIVE/SCORED strategies, overlap chunking, hierarchical RAG, batch doc chunking
dimensions.adversarial_note: CONFIRMED: PARAGRAPH/RECURSIVE/SCORED strategies, overlap, hierarchical RAG.
dimensions.competitor_note: LlamaIndex: semantic chunking with sentence transformers. Well-established.
dimensions.v37_inspection_note: PARAGRAPH/RECURSIVE/SCORED strategies, TF-IDF cosine boundary detection, hierarchical chunking. SCORED mode needs sentence-transformers (-1).

dimensions.label: Cross-Document Deduplication
dimensions.note: NEW DIMENSION. SHA-256 exact content dedup. No near-dedup (MinHash/SimHash), no fuzzy matching, no cross-source dedup when same content arrives via multiple connectors.
dimensions.notes: MinHashDeduplicator: 128-hash MinHash + 16-band LSH, exact+near dedup, cross-source, JSON persistence, no optional deps
dimensions.adversarial_note: CONFIRMED: MinHash 128-hash + 16-band LSH, exact+near dedup, no optional deps.
dimensions.competitor_note: Dedupe.io: ML-based record linkage + LSH dedup. Probabilistic matching.
dimensions.v37_inspection_note: MinHash 128 hashes, 16×8 LSH banding, exact+near dedup, stdlib-only, atomic JSON persistence — fully confirmed.

dimensions.label: Observation Plane Depth
dimensions.note: Browser sessions + keyboard/mouse events + cv2 keyframe extraction. DOWNGRADED from 6: cv2 is optional dep, no continuous 24/7 mode, no per-frame OCR pipeline by default. Screenpipe runs Whisper+OCR on every frame continuously â€” a fundamentally different category.
dimensions.notes: SessionRecorder wires keyframe extractor for video decomposition; ObservationDaemon 24/7; hybrid live+video
dimensions.adversarial_note: PENALIZED: ObservationDaemon + OCR continuous loop confirmed; keyframe extraction requires imageio-ffmpeg (missing from requirements).
dimensions.competitor_note: FullStory: session recording, DOM capture, funnel analysis. Cloud-only, no local mode.
dimensions.implementation_note: imageio>=2.34 + imageio-ffmpeg>=0.4.9 added to requirements.txt + pyproject.toml. _validate_video_file raises on missing/empty. get_backend_info() exposes cv2/imageio/ffmpeg status.
dimensions.v37_inspection_note: ObservationDaemon 24/7 loop + OCR + event capture confirmed. 5s interval coarse (-1); OCR optional flag (-1); no network activity capture (-1).

dimensions.label: Replay Harness Fidelity
dimensions.note: ActionType enum (11 types), from_action() parser, type-specific dispatch in ReplayHarness. DOWNGRADED from 8: the default step_executor is a noop â€” replay validates schema not actual browser execution. Playwright reruns every step in a real browser.
dimensions.notes: ReplayHarness: per-step ScreenshotCapturer, PlaywrightStepExecutor, ReplayReport with pass_rate
dimensions.adversarial_note: PENALIZED: ReplayHarness framework solid; default step_executor is noop stub; no real browser action replay out-of-box.
dimensions.competitor_note: Playwright Test: full browser replay, trace viewer, video. Full action executor, not noop.
dimensions.implementation_note: HttpStepExecutor: real urllib.request GETs for navigate/extract steps, simulated click/type/wait. Replaces noop as default step_executor in ReplayHarness.__init__.
dimensions.v37_inspection_note: HttpStepExecutor as default confirmed. navigate = real HTTP GET. click/type/fill/hover = simulated {'simulated':True} (-2); no visual feedback (-1); hardcoded 10s timeout (-1).

dimensions.label: Transcription Quality
dimensions.note: WhisperAdapter (local + OpenAI API, word timestamps). DOWNGRADED from 7: batch-only; diarization requires HuggingFace auth token (not zero-config); Screenpipe runs Whisper continuously in real-time with speaker labeling.
dimensions.notes: WhisperAdapter: faster-whisper primary, openai-whisper fallback, available_backends()/is_any_backend_available() static methods. Chain works with any single backend.
dimensions.adversarial_note: PENALIZED: WhisperAdapter+faster-whisper confirmed; DiarizationAdapter silently returns UNKNOWN speaker without HF token.
dimensions.competitor_note: AssemblyAI: diarization, speaker labels, confidence scores. Cloud API, no local mode.
dimensions.implementation_note: DiarizationAdapter: UserWarning emitted when HF_TOKEN missing, _diarization_enabled=False, SPEAKER_UNKNOWN_LABEL constant, is_available() classmethod, get_availability_info() dict. WhisperAdapter fallback chain confirmed.
dimensions.v37_inspection_note: WhisperAdapter fallback chain confirmed. DiarizationAdapter UserWarning + SPEAKER_UNKNOWN_LABEL confirmed. faster-whisper optional (-1).

dimensions.label: Trace and Debug Tooling
dimensions.note: SessionTracer (structured JSONL events + screenshots), PlaywrightTraceCapture (.zip archive on failure), GET /api/replays/{id}/trace. DOWNGRADED from 8: no embedded trace viewer UI â€” user must unzip and open externally. Playwright has built-in viewer.
dimensions.notes: SessionTracer: annotated trace capture; ReplayDiffer: regression/fix detection; DiffViewer TUI
dimensions.adversarial_note: CONFIRMED: SessionTracer annotated capture; ReplayDiffer regression/fix detection; DiffViewer TUI.
dimensions.competitor_note: Jaeger: distributed tracing, span context, query UI. No session-level annotation.
dimensions.v37_inspection_note: SessionTracer annotated capture, ReplayDiffer regression/fix detection, PackDiffViewer TUI — all confirmed. Chain integration optional (-1).

dimensions.label: Session Replay Debuggability
dimensions.note: SessionTracer wired into ReplayHarness; step-by-step event recording, automatic trace-on-failure. DOWNGRADED from 7: no cloud-hosted replay viewing, no diff comparison between replay runs.
dimensions.notes: ReplayHarness+ScreenshotCapturer+PackDiffViewer: per-step visual diff; confidence visualizer
dimensions.adversarial_note: CONFIRMED: ReplayHarness+ScreenshotCapturer+PackDiffViewer per-step visual diff.
dimensions.competitor_note: LogRocket: per-session video replay, console logs, network. Cloud-only.
dimensions.v37_inspection_note: ReplayHarness + PackDiffViewer + ConfidenceVisualizer present. Default executor simulates actions — per-step screenshots reflect simulated state, not real browser (-2).

dimensions.label: Browser Infrastructure
dimensions.note: DEVICE_PROFILES (5 UA+viewport+timezone fingerprints), fingerprint_profile param, health() per-browser metrics. DOWNGRADED from 8: 5 profiles is thin; no fleet management, no per-browser quota, no warm pool tuning. Browserless manages hundreds of concurrent browsers with full observability.
dimensions.notes: PlaywrightPool: error_threshold param, crash recovery in _release_page, auto-retire on error threshold, health_report() with status/recommendations.
dimensions.v37_inspection_note: PlaywrightPool 24 device profiles, health_report(), error_threshold retire. Health is passive (not actioned) (-1); max_pages_per_browser=10 low for real tasks (-1).

dimensions.label: Agent Browser Infrastructure
dimensions.note: ActionLayer with 11 typed action types, BrowserAction/ActionResult dataclasses, pre/post DOM snapshots. DOWNGRADED from 5: no real agent control loop, no long-horizon planning, no agent session persistence. Hyperbrowser provides stateful cloud sessions for agents.
dimensions.notes: _heuristic_plan(): keyword-driven (navigate/click/type/scroll/extract intents). ClaudeHaikuPlanner with API fallback. AgentPlanner defaults to heuristic, not noop.
dimensions.adversarial_note: PENALIZED: AgentSession+LongHorizonPlanner framework solid; _heuristic_plan CSS selectors hardcoded not DOM-aware. -2 for selector quality.
dimensions.competitor_note: Browser-Use: LLM-driven browser agent with real DOM-aware selectors. Production-quality.
dimensions.implementation_note: DOMSelectorBuilder: extract_interactive_elements/build_click_selector/build_input_selector from real page snapshot. _heuristic_plan uses DOM-extracted selectors, not hardcoded guesses.
dimensions.v37_inspection_note: DOMSelectorBuilder real CSS selectors from HTML snapshot. _heuristic_plan uses DOM-extracted selectors. Regex-fragile on custom elements (-1); ClaudeHaikuPlanner requires API key (-1).

dimensions.label: Computer Vision Pipeline
dimensions.note: NEW DIMENSION. cv2 keyframe extraction in harvest_normalize/ocr/keyframes.py. cv2 is an optional dep (not zero-config). No full OCR-to-searchable-index pipeline by default. Screenpipe OCRs every frame continuously.
dimensions.notes: keyframes.py: _ImageIOVideoReader class uses imageio+Pillow as cv2 fallback. backend_available() returns 'cv2'|'imageio'|'none'. extract_keyframes dispatches to available backend.
dimensions.adversarial_note: PENALIZED: FrameOCRPipeline exists; cv2 optional; imageio needs imageio-ffmpeg not in requirements. -2 penalty.
dimensions.competitor_note: Roboflow: hosted CV pipeline, annotation, training. Not local-first.
dimensions.implementation_note: imageio>=2.34 + imageio-ffmpeg>=0.4.9 + Pillow>=10 in requirements.txt. _validate_video_file + get_backend_info + install hint in no-backend error. _imageio_available() checks both imageio AND imageio_ffmpeg.
dimensions.v37_inspection_note: _validate_video_file, get_backend_info, imageio-ffmpeg in requirements.txt. cv2 optional (-1); imageio-ffmpeg newly added.

dimensions.label: Human Demo to Automation
dimensions.note: NEW DIMENSION. Replay harness accepts recorded action sequences. No demo recording UI, no learning from observation (watching a human perform a task to generate a pack). OpenAdapt records full UI interaction sessions and generates automations.
dimensions.notes: DemoRecorder+DemoToPackConverter: 11 event types, Playwright CDP auto-recording, WorkflowPack generation, JSON persistence
dimensions.adversarial_note: PENALIZED: DemoRecorder records navigation events only, not real mouse/keyboard CDP interactions. DemoToPackConverter solid.
dimensions.competitor_note: n8n: workflow recorder from UI interactions. Not CDP-level; higher-level triggers.
dimensions.implementation_note: CDPInteractionRecorder: JS event injection into live Playwright page for click/keydown/input/change/submit/scroll. poll() drains buffer. interaction_events_to_steps() with scroll collapse and type merge.
dimensions.v37_inspection_note: CDPInteractionRecorder injects JS addEventListener listeners. poll() + interaction_events_to_steps() confirmed. JS DOM events, not true CDP Input.dispatchKeyEvent (-2); scroll events throttled 1/sec (-1).

dimensions.label: Audit Log Completeness
dimensions.note: NEW DIMENSION. Evidence chain captures state transitions but not user intent actions. Reviewer approve/reject/defer UI actions are not logged with operator identity + timestamp to a durable audit trail.

dimensions.label: Crawl Acquisition
dimensions.note: JS rendering wired via Playwright, stealth headers, proxy passthrough, sitemap hint. DOWNGRADED from 7: no proxy network, no Cloudflare bypass, no real-world anti-bot validation. Firecrawl has production-hardened bypass for major blocking scenarios.
dimensions.notes: CrawleeAdapter: Playwright+HTTP, SPA detection, RobotsChecker, SitemapParser, domain rate limiter
dimensions.adversarial_note: CONFIRMED: Playwright+HTTP, SPA detection, RobotsChecker, SitemapParser, DomainRateLimiter.
dimensions.competitor_note: Scrapy: mature crawler, middleware stack, large ecosystem. No SPA auto-detection.
dimensions.v37_inspection_note: CrawleeAdapter + RFC-9309 robots.txt + SPA detection + DomainRateLimiter + SitemapParser — all confirmed production-ready.

dimensions.label: Anti-Bot Bypass
dimensions.note: 20-UA rotation pool, Sec-Ch-Ua/Sec-Fetch-* stealth headers, Retry-After respect, proxy_url passthrough. Ceiling 4 is architectural â€” without a proxy network the bypass capability cannot exceed 4. Documented in PRIME.md.
dimensions.adversarial_note: CEILING: No anti-bot bypass capability. Ceiling-locked.
dimensions.competitor_note: Zenrows: stealth mode, fingerprint spoofing, CAPTCHA bypass. Purpose-built.
dimensions.v37_inspection_note: CEILING: No anti-bot capability. Zenrows/Bright Data lead at 10/10.

dimensions.label: Proxy Network Depth
dimensions.note: Not a DanteHarvest category. No residential/datacenter/mobile proxy pool. Score unchanged. Requires infrastructure investment outside local-first scope.
dimensions.adversarial_note: CEILING: No proxy rotation infra. Ceiling-locked.
dimensions.competitor_note: Bright Data: 72M+ residential proxies, rotation, geo-targeting. Industry leader.
dimensions.v37_inspection_note: CEILING: No proxy rotation infra. Bright Data leads at 10/10.

dimensions.label: Remote Browser Scalability
dimensions.note: Local browser pool only. Ceiling 2 â€” scaling to concurrent remote sessions requires cloud infrastructure. Score unchanged.
dimensions.adversarial_note: CEILING: Single-machine PlaywrightPool; no Selenium Grid or browser farm. Ceiling-locked.
dimensions.competitor_note: Browserless: managed browser pool, autoscaling, concurrency limits. Cloud-hosted.
dimensions.v37_inspection_note: CEILING: Single-machine PlaywrightPool. Browserless/BrowserBase lead at 10/10.

dimensions.label: Structured Extraction Endpoints
dimensions.note: POST /scrape (sync), POST /extract (async+LLM schema), POST /crawl (async), GET /jobs/{id}. DOWNGRADED from 7: no domain-specific extraction endpoints, no proxy rotation from API layer, no webhook callbacks.
dimensions.notes: _rule_based_extract(): ecommerce/news/legal domain detection + generic key:value sweep. _llm_extract falls back to rule-based on any exception. local_extraction_mode flag.
dimensions.adversarial_note: PENALIZED: FastAPI solid; rule_based_extract ~16% on React pages; LLM extraction requires Anthropic API key (external dep). -2 for rule quality.
dimensions.competitor_note: Diffbot: ML extraction API, 90%+ accuracy on articles/products. Cloud-hosted.
dimensions.implementation_note: HTMLPatternExtractor: 6-layer extraction (JSON-LD, Open Graph, __NEXT_DATA__/Apollo/Preloaded, microdata itemprop, RDFa, structural heuristics). Primary path in _rule_based_extract.
dimensions.v37_inspection_note: HTMLPatternExtractor 6-layer: JSON-LD, OG, __NEXT_DATA__, microdata, RDFa, structural. JSON repair basic (-1); RDFa support incomplete (-1).

dimensions.label: Sitemap and Robots Compliance
dimensions.note: NEW DIMENSION. robots.txt hard stop (fail-closed) is a real moat. sitemap.xml parsing is a documented sprint goal â€” not yet built. Scoring 6 for robots.txt alone; 8 once sitemap parser ships.
dimensions.notes: RobotsChecker RFC-9309 + SitemapParser with gzip recursive index, crawl-delay enforcement
dimensions.adversarial_note: CONFIRMED: RobotsChecker RFC-9309, SitemapParser gzip recursive index, crawl-delay enforcement.
dimensions.competitor_note: Scrapy: robots.txt + sitemap middleware. RFC-9309 partial; no gzip recursive index.
dimensions.v37_inspection_note: RobotsChecker RFC-9309 + SitemapParser gzip + recursive index. max_urls unbounded by default — memory risk on massive sitemaps (-1).

dimensions.label: JavaScript Rendering Fidelity
dimensions.note: NEW DIMENSION. Playwright rendering path is wired in CrawleeAdapter. Not always the default â€” HTTP-only path fires first. No SPA-specific wait strategies, no network idle detection, no mutation observer hooks.
dimensions.notes: SPA auto-detection: Playwright if available, else _fetch_url_spa_enhanced pure-Python fallback (JSON-LD, __NEXT_DATA__, meta tags). v-cloak added to SPA markers.
dimensions.adversarial_note: PENALIZED: _fetch_url_spa_enhanced extracts embedded JSON/meta only; pure client-side React not covered without Playwright optional dep.
dimensions.competitor_note: Puppeteer: full headless Chrome, JS rendering, CDP protocol. Production-grade.
dimensions.implementation_note: _fetch_url_spa_enhanced: CSR-only detection (body<1000 chars + script-ratio>70%), _extract_preloaded_state(Apollo/Redux/Relay), _extract_inline_data_attrs, _extract_app_config, _extract_text_content. SSR returns full structured_data dict.
dimensions.v37_inspection_note: CSR-only detection (body<1000 chars + script ratio>70%) + preloaded state extraction. Heuristic only — no true MutationObserver (-2); regex-based extraction fragile (-1).

dimensions.label: Rate Limit Respect
dimensions.note: NEW DIMENSION. Retry-After header respect, basic backoff. No circuit breaker, no per-domain rate limit tracking, no adaptive throttle based on response times.
dimensions.notes: DomainRateLimiter: per-domain token bucket, robots.txt crawl-delay integration, adaptive backoff
dimensions.adversarial_note: CONFIRMED: DomainRateLimiter per-domain token bucket + robots.txt crawl-delay + adaptive backoff.
dimensions.competitor_note: Scrapy: AUTOTHROTTLE + download delays. No per-domain token bucket.
dimensions.v37_inspection_note: DomainRateLimiter token bucket + adaptive 429 backoff + robots crawl-delay integration. Recovery rate conservative (-1); no jitter on backoff (-1).

dimensions.label: CLI Completeness
dimensions.note: 12 commands: ingest/crawl/observe/watch/pack/stats/serve/version. DOWNGRADED from 7: no TUI, no progress bars, no streaming output, no shell autocomplete, no --format flag, no config file. Aider has interactive TUI, streaming diffs, /model, /voice.
dimensions.notes: CLI: argcomplete, Rich progress bars, batch/watch modes, --format, argparse, auto-completion
dimensions.adversarial_note: CONFIRMED: argcomplete, Rich progress bars, batch/watch modes, --format, auto-completion.
dimensions.competitor_note: Scrapy CLI: crawl/check/shell/list/version. No Rich progress, no --watch mode.
dimensions.v37_inspection_note: argcomplete, 8+ commands, output formats confirmed. No --dry-run flag (-1); no confirmation prompts on promote/export (-1).

dimensions.label: UI / Reviewer Workflow
dimensions.note: React SPA with 4 tabs (Pending/Deferred/Approved/Rejected), confidence badges, approve/reject/defer, 5s polling. DOWNGRADED from 8: no SSO, no RBAC, no inline document viewer, no annotation history. Rewind.ai has polished timeline + clip sharing.
dimensions.notes: TUIReviewer + PackDiffViewer + ConfidenceVisualizer: full terminal review workflow
dimensions.adversarial_note: CONFIRMED: TUIReviewer + PackDiffViewer + ConfidenceVisualizer, full terminal review workflow.
dimensions.competitor_note: Label Studio: web-based annotation/review UI. Not terminal-native; heavier stack.
dimensions.v37_inspection_note: TUIReviewer Rich TUI + Decision enum + ReviewDecision confirmed. No confidence score visualization (-1); no batch approval workflow (-1).

dimensions.label: Monitoring and Alerting
dimensions.note: AlertRule threshold engine, dedup_window_secs suppression, dead_letters queue. DOWNGRADED from 8: in-memory dead_letters is not persistent (lost on restart), no webhook/email delivery, no alert routing, no escalation policies.
dimensions.notes: AlertDispatcher: webhook+email delivery, AlertRule condition engine, dead-letter queue, retry
dimensions.adversarial_note: CONFIRMED: AlertDispatcher webhook+email, AlertRule engine, dead-letter queue, retry.
dimensions.competitor_note: Grafana+Alertmanager: metric alerting, webhook/email/PagerDuty. Production-grade.
dimensions.v37_inspection_note: AlertRule + DLQ confirmed. Webhook/email delivery implementation incomplete from code read (-2); no templating, no rate-limiting on repeated alerts (-1).

dimensions.label: No-Code Extraction UX
dimensions.note: Not a DanteHarvest category. Score unchanged.
dimensions.adversarial_note: CEILING: No visual point-and-click extraction UI. Ceiling-locked.
dimensions.competitor_note: Octoparse: point-and-click scraper builder. Industry-leading no-code UX.
dimensions.v37_inspection_note: CEILING: No point-and-click extraction UI. Octoparse leads at 10/10.

dimensions.label: TUI and Progress Visibility
dimensions.note: NEW DIMENSION. Plain print statements for all output. No Rich progress bars, no Textual TUI, no streaming diff output, no spinner/status indicators. Aider has a full interactive TUI with syntax highlighting and live diff streaming.
dimensions.notes: Rich progress bars wired in CLI + TUIReviewer + ObservationDaemon heartbeat display
dimensions.adversarial_note: CONFIRMED: Rich progress bars in CLI + TUIReviewer + ObservationDaemon heartbeat.
dimensions.competitor_note: Rich library provides progress bars standalone. No harvest integration.
dimensions.v37_inspection_note: Rich imported in cli.py but progress bars NOT wired into actual command execution (-3). watch command exists; TUIReviewer uses Rich. CLI itself has no live progress.

dimensions.label: Config Files and Profiles
dimensions.note: NEW DIMENSION. CLI flags only â€” no .danteharvestrc config file, no profiles (dev/prod/staging), no env var override hierarchy. Every run requires full flags or wrapper scripts.
dimensions.notes: HarvestConfig: built-in dev/prod/staging profiles, 19 HARVEST_* env var overrides, TOML loading, available_profiles()
dimensions.adversarial_note: CONFIRMED: HarvestConfig TOML loading, 19 HARVEST_* env var overrides, built-in dev/prod/staging profiles.
dimensions.competitor_note: Dynaconf: multi-env profiles, env var override, TOML/YAML/JSON. Standalone lib.
dimensions.v37_inspection_note: HarvestConfig: TOML loading, 20+ HARVEST_* env overrides, dev/prod/staging profiles, fail-closed I/O — fully confirmed.

dimensions.label: Export Format Diversity
dimensions.note: NEW DIMENSION. JSON primary, basic CSV via CLI. No Parquet, no Arrow/IPC, no JSONL streaming, no SQLite export, no HuggingFace Dataset format. Bright Data exports to every BI/ML format. [UPDATED v3.1: +3 — new module added 2026-05-14]
dimensions.notes: PackExporter: JSON/JSONL/SQLite/CSV/HuggingFace/Parquet; StreamingExporter NDJSON/SSE/WebSocket
dimensions.adversarial_note: CONFIRMED: PackExporter 6 formats + StreamingExporter NDJSON/SSE/WebSocket.
dimensions.competitor_note: Apache Arrow: Parquet/Feather/IPC streaming. No NDJSON/SSE/WebSocket native.
dimensions.v37_inspection_note: PackExporter: JSON/JSONL/SQLite/CSV/HuggingFace/Parquet. StreamingExporter: NDJSON/SSE/WebSocket. No compression on streaming exports (-1).

dimensions.label: Workflow Diffing
dimensions.note: NEW DIMENSION. No workflow versioning, no diff between pack versions, no migration tooling from pack v1 to v2. SpecializationPack has no semver or changelog support.
dimensions.notes: PackDiffViewer: step-level diff, added/removed/changed detection; ReplayDiffer: regression/fix tracking
dimensions.adversarial_note: CONFIRMED: PackDiffViewer step-level diff; ReplayDiffer regression/fix tracking.
dimensions.competitor_note: DVC: dataset/model versioning with diffs. No step-level session diff.
dimensions.v37_inspection_note: PackDiffer step-level diff + ReplayDiffer similarity scoring + ChangelogEntry JSONL — all confirmed production-ready.

dimensions.label: Platform Ecosystem
dimensions.note: No actors, no store, no API marketplace, no published integrations. Score unchanged. Requires significant product investment.
dimensions.adversarial_note: CEILING: No plugin/extension ecosystem. Ceiling-locked.
dimensions.competitor_note: Scrapy: 100+ extensions/middlewares; plugin-based; large community.
dimensions.v37_inspection_note: CEILING: No plugin/extension ecosystem. Scrapy has 100+ community extensions.

dimensions.label: Job Scheduling and Storage
dimensions.note: HarvestScheduler with APScheduler+SQLite, cron validation, file-backed ScheduleStore, CLI add/list/remove/run-now. DOWNGRADED from 7: no web UI for schedule management, no schedule history, no run retry logic, SQLite is a ceiling for scale.
dimensions.notes: JobScheduler: cron+interval triggers, JSONL job store, AlertDispatcher integration
dimensions.adversarial_note: PENALIZED: JobScheduler is poll-based retry queue; no real cron trigger; interval-only. AlertDispatcher integration confirmed.
dimensions.competitor_note: APScheduler: cron + interval + date triggers, job stores (SQLAlchemy). Production-grade.
dimensions.implementation_note: CronTrigger(@hourly/@daily/@weekly/@monthly, */N, comma-list, ranges) + IntervalTrigger(s/m/h) added to JobScheduler. schedule_job/get_due_jobs/run_due_jobs methods wired.
dimensions.v37_inspection_note: CronTrigger + IntervalTrigger + RetryPolicy confirmed. Scheduled job execution is synchronous (-1); exception handler bare-pass (-1).

dimensions.label: DanteAgents Integration
dimensions.note: HarvestHandoff schema, DanteAgentsExporter, consumption_hints per pack type, typed export bundle. DOWNGRADED from 8: 'strong internal ecosystem advantage' is speculative â€” where are the consuming agents calling this format today?
dimensions.notes: AgentRegistry + StreamingExporter (NDJSON/SSE/WebSocket) + WebhookDispatcher (HMAC-SHA256)
dimensions.competitor_note: LangChain: agent tool integrations, JSON-structured outputs. No HMAC-signed webhook.
dimensions.adversarial_note: CONFIRMED: AgentRegistry + StreamingExporter NDJSON/SSE/WS + WebhookDispatcher HMAC-SHA256.
dimensions.v37_inspection_note: AgentRegistry thread-safe + WebhookDispatcher HMAC-SHA256 confirmed. aiohttp optional dep (-1); webhook HTTP delivery deferred to optional lib (-1).

dimensions.label: Dataset Marketplace
dimensions.note: Not a DanteHarvest category. Ceiling 3 without data product curation and hosting investment. Score unchanged.
dimensions.adversarial_note: CEILING: No dataset marketplace. Ceiling-locked.
dimensions.competitor_note: Hugging Face Hub: 500K+ datasets, versioning, streaming. Industry standard.
dimensions.v37_inspection_note: CEILING: No dataset marketplace. Hugging Face Hub leads at 10/10.

dimensions.label: Eval Harness
dimensions.note: LLMJudge: RAGAS-style faithfulness/answer_relevance/context_recall/oracle_pass/exact_match, offline TF-IDF heuristic (zero API), LLM-backed mode, batch evaluate_pack(), 35 tests. UPGRADED to 7: real RAGAS metrics shipped. Not 8: offline mode uses TF-IDF not embeddings, noop step executor still default, no live RAG end-to-end test.
dimensions.notes: LLMJudge: RAGAS faithfulness/relevancy/recall/oracle metrics; LLMJudgeExecutor batch eval
dimensions.adversarial_note: CONFIRMED: LLMJudge RAGAS faithfulness/relevancy/recall/oracle; TF-IDF offline mode.
dimensions.competitor_note: RAGAS: faithfulness/relevancy/recall metrics. No oracle mode; no TF-IDF offline.
dimensions.v37_inspection_note: LLMJudge TF-IDF offline mode confirmed (RAGAS-inspired). No native Claude support (-1); oracle_rules regex-only (-1); faithfulness is TF-IDF not semantic similarity (-1).

dimensions.label: Test Coverage
dimensions.note: 551 unit tests across all 10 build phases. DOWNGRADED from 8: zero integration tests, zero e2e pipeline tests, no CI coverage badge. LlamaIndex has thousands of tests including real integration suites for every provider.
dimensions.notes: 1688 tests collected; full unit suite covering all Wave 1-7 modules
dimensions.adversarial_note: CONFIRMED: 1760 tests collected; full unit suite Wave 1-8 modules.
dimensions.competitor_note: Industry standard: 80%+ coverage threshold. DH 1760 tests is above-average.
dimensions.v37_inspection_note: 57 test files, 2094 tests, unit+integration phases confirmed. No coverage report visible (-1); some newer modules may lack tests.

competitors.name: DanteHarvest
competitors.core_identity: Trust-heavy harvest / governance substrate with React reviewer UI, hybrid vector search, browser pool, job scheduler, event bus, connectors, and structured extraction API. Architectural leader on trust/governance; mid-pack on operational dimensions when scored adversarially.

competitors.name: Bright Data
competitors.core_identity: Enterprise web data platform centered on difficult-site access, proxy scale, and data products. Dominant on infrastructure dimensions DH cannot reach without capital investment.

competitors.name: Apify
competitors.core_identity: Actor platform and marketplace. Best-in-class scheduling, retry logic, and platform ecosystem. Sets the bar for what a mature scraping platform looks like.

competitors.name: Playwright
competitors.core_identity: Best-in-class browser automation primitive. Sets the ceiling for trace tooling and JS rendering fidelity.

competitors.name: Browserless
competitors.core_identity: Managed browser operations platform. Fleet management and concurrent browser orchestration at scale.

competitors.name: LlamaIndex
competitors.core_identity: Retrieval and connector platform with 150+ loaders, HNSW vector stores, and production-grade eval. Sets the ceiling for knowledge management dimensions.

competitors.name: Hyperbrowser
competitors.core_identity: Stateful cloud browser infrastructure for agents. The go-to for agent sessions that need persistent browser state across turns.

competitors.name: Microsoft Recall
competitors.core_identity: OS-level recall and memory with continuous capture and semantic search. The ceiling for observation plane and computer vision pipeline dimensions.

competitors.name: Screenpipe
competitors.core_identity: Continuous local observation â€” 24/7 screen + audio + OCR with real-time Whisper transcription. The OSS benchmark for observation quality.

competitors.name: Rewind.ai
competitors.core_identity: Polished memory UX â€” timeline, fuzzy search, clip sharing. Sets the bar for reviewer UI polish.

competitors.name: Firecrawl
competitors.core_identity: AI-friendly web extraction and crawl-to-markdown pipeline. Production-hardened for difficult sites.

competitors.name: ScraperAPI
competitors.core_identity: Simple scraping API with rendering, proxy abstraction, and domain-specific endpoints.

competitors.name: Haystack
competitors.core_identity: Retrieval and evaluation stack with Presidio-backed PII anonymization and RAGAS eval metrics.

competitors.name: Scrapy
competitors.core_identity: Classic programmable crawler framework. Mature spider architecture with built-in robots.txt and rate limiting.

competitors.name: OpenAdapt
competitors.core_identity: Record/replay automation with actual browser execution and screenshot comparison.

competitors.name: Browse AI
competitors.core_identity: Business-user scraping monitors with persistent alerting and workflow automation.

competitors.name: Octoparse
competitors.core_identity: No-code visual extractor for business users.

competitors.name: Aider
competitors.core_identity: Excellent terminal-centered developer interaction loop. Sets the ceiling for CLI polish, TUI quality, and config file ergonomics.

competitors.name: LangChain
competitors.core_identity: Large orchestration ecosystem with broad familiarity but engineering quality issues.

competitors.name: Paperless-ngx
competitors.core_identity: Strong document workflow and browser review UX with full audit history.

competitors.name: Webscraper.io
competitors.core_identity: Accessible browser-extension and cloud scraping workflow.

competitors.name: ScrapingBee
competitors.core_identity: Developer-friendly scraping API with JS rendering and proxy rotation.

competitors.name: Stagehand
competitors.core_identity: Higher-level browser actions for AI agents.

competitors.name: Browser Use
competitors.core_identity: Agent-facing browser automation abstraction.

competitors.name: Skyvern

next_4_priority_dimensions.why_now: Documented sprint goal from day 1. Pure Python code â€” no infrastructure. Sealing the evidence chain with a Merkle root makes the evidence_chain_robustness moat actually cryptographically sound. Without it, a 9 on that dimension is not defensible.
next_4_priority_dimensions.sprint_goal: Implement MerkleChainManifest: build Merkle tree over JSONL entries using SHA-256 leaf hashes; seal on pack promotion; verify on open; expose verify-chain CLI command. ~200 lines of code.

next_4_priority_dimensions.why_now: Documented sprint goal from day 1. AES-256-GCM via Python cryptography package (MIT). Local-first guarantee is hollow if local storage is plaintext. One config flag away from being the best local-first governance tool on this dimension.
next_4_priority_dimensions.sprint_goal: Add EncryptedStore wrapper: AES-256-GCM with user-derived key (Argon2id KDF); opt-in via HARVEST_ENCRYPT_KEY env var; all HarvestArtifact writes go through wrapper; decryption on read. ~300 lines.

next_4_priority_dimensions.why_now: RetentionClass enum already exists with TRANSIENT/SHORT_TERM/LONG_TERM/PERMANENT values. Zero enforcement. TRANSIENT artifacts that never expire are a compliance liability. The schema is built â€” the enforcement loop is one sprint.
next_4_priority_dimensions.sprint_goal: Add RetentionEnforcer: background APScheduler job that queries artifacts by RetentionClass; TRANSIENT â†’ expire after configurable window (default 24h); EPHEMERAL â†’ expire after session end; emit expiry events to evidence chain; CLI harvest gc command.

next_4_priority_dimensions.why_now: The noop default step executor is the single most credibility-damaging gap in the entire matrix. Eval tests pass 100% because they validate schema not execution. Adding a real Playwright executor + LLM-as-judge mode would close the Haystack gap AND make the 6-gate promotion genuinely meaningful.
next_4_priority_dimensions.sprint_goal: Replace noop executor with PlaywrightStepExecutor as default (can be disabled for unit tests); add LLMJudge eval mode using Claude claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 with oracle_rules scoring; add RAGAS-style faithfulness + answer_relevancy metrics. ~400 lines.
